reasoning: The Lean statement universally quantifies (via typeclasses) over compact metric spaces K and asserts the existence of a set B of subsets of K with `Set.Countable B ∧ IsTopologicalBasis B`. This exactly matches "K has a countable base": `Set.Countable` is the standard Mathlib notion of countability (allowing finite or countably infinite), and `IsTopologicalBasis` (from `Mathlib.Topology.Bases`) is the standard notion of a topological basis for the ambient topology, which here is the metric topology induced by `[MetricSpace K]`. The conclusion is equivalent to `SecondCountableTopology K`. No hypotheses are added or removed relative to the informal claim.
